seoTitle: How to Adjust Line and Paragraph Spacing in Google Docs
metaDescription: Change line spacing and paragraph gaps in Google Docs using preset options or custom point values, and save your settings as the default.
intro: Fixing inconsistent line spacing or preparing a document for an academic style guide shouldn't mean hitting the return key over and over. This guide covers Google Docs' spacing presets, custom spacing controls, and how to save your preferred settings as the default.

steps.heading: Select your text
steps.body: Highlight the paragraphs you want to adjust, or press Ctrl+A (or Cmd+A) to select the entire document.

steps.heading: Open the spacing tool
steps.body: Click the line and paragraph spacing icon in the toolbar to reveal preset options like single, 1.15, 1.5, and double spacing.

steps.heading: Apply a preset
steps.body: Choose one of the standard spacing presets to instantly update the selected text.

steps.heading: Set custom spacing values
steps.body: Reopen the spacing menu, click Custom Spacing, and enter exact point values for line height and paragraph padding.

steps.heading: Save your styles as default
steps.body: Go to Format, hover over Paragraph Styles, and click 'Save as My Default Styles' so future documents use the same spacing.

faq.question: What's the difference between line spacing and paragraph spacing?
faq.answer: Line spacing controls the gap within a paragraph's lines, while paragraph spacing controls the gap before or after entire paragraphs.

faq.question: Can I remove extra space between paragraphs without changing line spacing?
faq.answer: Yes, toggle 'Remove space after paragraph' in the spacing menu to tighten gaps without affecting line height.

faq.question: Does changing spacing affect the whole document automatically?
faq.answer: Only the text you have selected will change, so select the entire document if you want a uniform update.

faq.question: Will my custom spacing carry over to new documents?
faq.answer: Only if you save it as your default style; otherwise each new document starts with standard spacing.
